subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Inflation: The Universe's Growth Spurt
description: A fast breakdown of Inflation: The Universe's Growth Spurt for builders.

hook.text: In a tiny fraction of a second after the Big Bang, the universe expanded faster than the speed of light.

buildup.text: This era of cosmic inflation lasted about a trillionth of a trillionth of a trillionth of a second.

discovery.text: Alan Guth proposed inflation in 1980 to explain why the universe looks the same in every direction.

twist.text: Without inflation, opposite sides of the sky would have never been in contact—yet they share the same temperature.

climax.text: Inflation stretched tiny quantum fluctuations into the seeds of galaxies. Randomness became cosmic structure.

punchline.text: A burst faster than light. It shaped every galaxy we see.

quiz.question: What problem did cosmic inflation solve?
options: Why the universe looks the same temperature in every direction
Why the Big Bang happened at all
Why galaxies are moving apart from each other
